words: This interview with Nellie Wood Smith took place at her home at Bovill on
September 10, 1975. The interviewer is Sam Schrager.
SAM SCHRAGER:  Do you remember much of what Bovill was like in that very first days
that you were
here? What were the first impressions you had?
NELLIE WOOD:  Well, I think about the first thing I remember then why I said, "What's
that they're
building?" "Well, that's the depot." So, the road used to come in instead of coming
right straight up this hill from Main Street on from this street, I mean, straight
up
the hill across the track, why it used to make a turn up there just - oh, - just not
far from the bridge, about even with that cedar mill, you know, across the road there.
The road used to turn to the left and go over almost to those big barns there.
And then it came right straight down Main Street coming into town. The road has been
changed since that. And when it came into there right straight across the meadow,
you
see. The first good look you got of what was supposed to be the town was - they were
just building the depot. It was, oh, there was a barber shop here and there was a
men's clothing store - it was a little bit of everything - but it was supposed to
be
lumberjacks' supplies mostly. They had their logging boots and things of that sort
and
tin pans. Mr. Parker had his - E.K. Parker from Princeton - had his store, that
men's clothing, and then A.A.Anderson, he was from Tennessee. And he was
running the barber shop. Well, there was a restaurant; I think the restaurant was
in working order at that time. He was one of the early ones anyway. But I don't know
for sure. By the name of Lester Smith was running the eating place.
